sanskrit: समोऽहं सर्वभूतेषु न मे द्वेष्योऽस्ति न प्रियः । ये भजन्ति तु मां भक्त्या मयि ते तेषु चाप्यहम् ॥
hindiTranslation: मैं सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंमें समान हूँ ।  उन प्राणियोंमें न तो कोई मेरा द्वेषी है और न कोई प्रिय है ।  परन्तु जो भक्तिपूर्वक मेरा भजन करते हैं, वे मेरेमें हैं और मैं उनमें हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: I am equal toward all beings. No one is hated by me, and no one is especially dear. But those who worship me with devotion dwell in me, and I dwell in them.
essence: Equality is universal; intimacy is born through devotion.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: The divine is not partial or biased. Everyone is met equally, but loving devotion creates a living closeness between the divine and the devotee.
simpleInsight: You do not have to earn basic worth; devotion changes distance into intimacy.
detailedMeaning: Krishna is still speaking on the battlefield to Arjuna, after teaching him to offer every action to the divine. Now he clarifies a subtle point: divine presence is equal everywhere, yet sincere devotion creates a mutual indwelling that transforms relationship.
modernRelevance: You send the message, then wait for the reply that might never come. This verse says the deepest presence is not bought by approval; it grows where devotion is real.
translationLiteral: समः (equal) / अहम् (I) / सर्वभूतेषु (toward all beings) / न (not) / मे (to me) / द्वेष्यः (hated one) / अस्ति (is) / न (not) / प्रियः (dear one) / ये (those who) / भजन्ति (worship) / तु (but) / माम् (me) / भक्त्या (with devotion) / मयि (in me) / ते (they) / तेषु (in them) / च (and) / अपि (also) / अहम् (I)
insight: It shifts the mind from trying to impress the divine to entering a relationship of mutual presence.

quoteVariants.text: I hold all beings equally. Devotion makes you dwell in me, and me in you.
Equal to all. Near to the devoted.
No one is farther from me than anyone else.
